hlsCompetition.title: Universal FPGA Compiler
hlsCompetition.sub: Write code in ANY programming language. Compile to hardware. No vendor lock-in.
hlsCompetition.problemDesc: Today, to compile code to FPGA hardware, companies pay $3,000-$50,000/year for tools that only support C/C++. Developers must rewrite everything. We eliminate this barrier.

advantages.title: Write Python, Deploy Hardware
advantages.desc: Your Python/JS/Rust code compiles directly to FPGA. No C++ rewrite needed.

advantages.title: $0 vs $50,000/year
advantages.desc: Enterprise HLS tools cost $50K+. VIBEE is free and open source forever.

advantages.title: No Vendor Lock-in
advantages.desc: Same code works on AMD, Intel, Lattice FPGAs. Switch vendors anytime.

advantages.title: 10-100x Faster Development
advantages.desc: Skip the C++ rewrite. Go from idea to hardware in hours, not months.

hlsCompetition.whyItMattersDesc: FPGAs are 10-100x more energy efficient than GPUs for AI inference. But adoption is blocked by expensive, C-only tools. VIBEE removes this barrier — opening a $23B market to every developer.
hlsCompetition.note: VIBEE is the world's first multi-language FPGA compiler. Open source. Free forever.

mysticism.title: Mathematical Foundations
mysticism.subtitle: Why 3? Inside every proton and neutron, quarks have exactly 3 'colors' — this is SU(3) symmetry that holds atoms together. Our ternary system {-1, 0, +1} mirrors this natural pattern. Information theory also proves: optimal number base is e ≈ 2.718, nearest integer = 3.

items.title: SU(3) — Special Unitary Group
items.description: SU = Special Unitary (matrices with determinant = 1 that preserve vector length). SU(3) describes 3 quark colors (red, green, blue) inside protons and neutrons. Our ternary {-1, 0, +1} mirrors this natural 3-state pattern. The 8 gluon generators provide natural error correction.

items.title: Chern-Simons Invariants
items.description: Topological protection of quantum states through Chern-Simons theory. The invariant k=3 corresponds to ternary logic depth.

items.title: Golden Ratio Identity
items.description: The mathematical identity φ² + 1/φ² = 3 connects golden ratio to ternary. This identity underlies optimal information encoding.

items.title: Optimal Radix Theorem
items.description: Information theory proves: the most efficient number base is e ≈ 2.718. The closest integer is 3, making ternary 1.58× more efficient than binary.

sacredFormula.title: Sacred Formula Engine
sacredFormula.description: Integer Relation Detection: every physical constant as a point in the lattice spanned by {1, log(3), log(π), log(φ)} over ℤ.
sacredFormula.predictionsDisclaimer: NOT established physics — experimental mathematics only
sacredFormula.howItWorks: Any number can be written as a simple formula from 4 constants of nature. For example, electron mass = 1 × 3⁻⁷ × π¹ × φ⁻² × e³ with 0.003% accuracy. This isn't magic — it's an integer lattice. Enter your number and see its "coordinates" in this lattice.
sacredFormula.tryIt: Enter a number → get its formula
